minutes_briefs.title: Clean Energy Act deadline extended to April 1, 2029
minutes_briefs.summary: The BC government has pushed back a key deadline under the Clean Energy Act. Originally, certain rules were set to take effect on April 1, 2026. That date has now been moved to April 1, 2029. This gives businesses an extra three years to prepare for the changes. The specific rules being delayed are in sections (c) and (d) of B.C. Reg. 185/2023. If your business is affected by those sections, you now have more time to comply.
minutes_briefs.action_required: No immediate action is needed, but review B.C. Reg. 185/2023 to see if sections (c) and (d) apply to your business.

key_dates.label: Clean Energy Act compliance deadline (sections c & d) takes effect

minutes_briefs.source_body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 29/2026
Made under: Clean Energy Act
Published in: No. 5 – B.C. Regs. 29 to 42, March 24, 2026
Deposit date: March 11, 2026

B.C. Reg. 29/2026, deposited March 11, 2026, under theCLEAN ENERGY ACT[section 38 (b)]. Regulation of Treasury Board, dated March 10, 2026. Treasury Board orders that B.C. Reg. 185/2023 is amended in paragraphs (c) and (d) by striking out "April 1, 2026" and substituting "April 1, 2029". — B. BAILEY,Chair, Treasury Board.
